MATTER WAS HEARD AND THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE OF CCP 36(D), LAST DAY 7/13/2010. THE HEARING IS CONTINUED FROM MAR-15-2010 TO MAR-19-2010, 2:00 PM IN DEPT. 220 TO SET TRIAL DATE AND DISCUSS ALL SCHEDULING ISSUES FOR TRIAL. PLAINTIFF WILL E-MAIL DISCOVERY RESPONSES TO DEFENDANTS WHO ANSWERED; IDENTIFY PERSONS MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE OF FACTS. THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF'S FATHER WILL BE ADVANCED AND TAKEN THIS WEEK. PLAINTIFF WILL MEET AND CONFER WITH DEFENDANTS TO WORK ON ALL DETAILS OF RECORDS. COURT WILL SCHEDULE A SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE DATE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: SCOTT HUGHES, CSR # 12365

Kimberly Chu, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Joseph Fernandez

RULING:
DEFENDANT SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Sequoia Ventures Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Fernandez was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's failure to file and serve on plaintiffs its declaration and supporting evidence precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Mary Ann Scanlan, CSR # 8875 = (503/TLJ)
